title: SpaceX shares plummet below IPO price and lose one trillion dollars
summary: The space manufacturer suffers a sharp stock market correction, with its bonds approaching 'junk' status and investor confidence deteriorated.

title: Plunge in Asian markets over fear of chip oversupply after CXMT's IPO announcement
summary: The entry of a new Chinese memory manufacturer triggers a wave of selling in semiconductor stocks, with SK Hynix falling 11% and the Nikkei losing nearly 2,000 points.

title: European stocks are indecisive and the Dax fails to reach 25,000 points
summary: Caution over geopolitical tensions and anticipation of corporate earnings keep European indices without a clear direction.

title: Uber launches a cash takeover bid for Delivery Hero worth $14.8 billion
summary: The mobility company offers 41.50 euros per share to acquire the food delivery platform.

title: TSMC's profit increases 77% in the second quarter due to high demand for high-end chips
summary: The Taiwanese semiconductor company reports revenue of $40.2 billion and reaffirms its leadership in the sector.

title: ASML raises its 2026 sales forecast to $45 billion thanks to AI
summary: The lithography equipment maker earns 27% more in the second quarter and improves its financial outlook.

title: Stripe and Advent launch a buyout offer for PayPal valued at $53 billion
summary: The potential acquisition sends shares of the payments platform soaring and points to consolidation in the fintech sector.

title: BlackRock becomes the world's first asset manager to surpass $15 trillion in assets
summary: The largest investment firm reaches an all-time high driven by capital inflows and the market rally.

title: United States attacks an oil tanker heading to Kharg Island and tensions in Hormuz push oil prices up
summary: The escalation in the Strait of Hormuz leads traders to warn of a market on the brink of exhaustion and an expensive winter.

title: Wall Street closes positive with fintech and tech stocks leading
summary: The main US indices end the session in the green, highlighting the rise of fintech sector stocks.

title: UK economy grows 0.1% in May and emerges from technical contraction
summary: The British GDP data shows a slight recovery after the recession period.

title: Bank of Korea raises interest rates to 2.75% for the first time in three years
summary: The monetary authority tightens its policy in response to inflationary pressures and to stabilize the economy.

title: ECB board member Nagel considers current rates 'adequate' and supports a pause in July
summary: Joachim Nagel points out that the rate level is appropriate, suggesting that they will not raise them at the next meeting.

title: Fed's Beige Book shows slight economic improvement and moderation of inflation
summary: The Federal Reserve report indicates moderate growth in activity and a decrease in price pressures.

title: United States imposes 25% tariff on imports from Brazil
summary: The US government applies the measure in response to Brazil's lack of sincere negotiation, according to Rubio.

title: EU prepares crisis team for potential disruption of Chinese rare earth supply
summary: Brussels activates a contingency plan to deal with a possible blockade by China in the context of the trade war.

title: Patrick Drahi's Altice International accused of defaulting on €2 billion in debt
summary: The telecom group's subsidiary faces accusations of default, increasing pressure on the French businessman.

title: Thames Water warns about its future viability as government considers intervention
summary: The British water company warns of financial problems and Mayor Burnham proposes nationalization of the service.

title: Digi starts trading on the Spanish stock exchange at €5.60 per share
summary: The telecom operator debuts on the stock market with strong institutional reception and triggers a downgrade of Telefónica by Barclays.

title: Energy company IPOs surge amid the rise of artificial intelligence
summary: Investor appetite for companies leveraging AI for the energy transition drives a wave of IPOs in the sector.
